behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure is at the boundary between manifest validation and module-loading (must prove reject happens before import side effects), not just pure schema logic. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess is required; local fixture files are enough. MOCK-HIDE: mocking import/loader could falsely pass and miss accidental early execution. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because behavior is in-process loader sequencing with local filesystem only.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with one invalid plugin whose entry module writes a sentinel file or mutates shared state on import. Run the real loader against that directory. Assert: validation error is reported for missing required manifest field, sentinel side effect never occurs, and plugin onLoad is never called.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's onLoad hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bug appears when dependency resolver output is handed to lifecycle executor. Unit-testing topo-sort alone misses hook-call ordering integration. REAL-INFRA: no subprocess/network needed. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing either resolver or executor can hide a bad handoff. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because this is module orchestration in one process.
behaviors.test_description: Use two real fixture plugins A->B with onLoad hooks appending to a shared in-memory/event log. Run loader and assert observed order is [B.onLoad, A.onLoad].

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior combines cycle detection with load suppression (no plugin loaded). Unit graph test alone does not verify loader actually blocks execution. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess dependence beyond normal in-process execution. MOCK-HIDE: mocked loader stages can miss accidental partial loads. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified since no external process or network behavior is required.
behaviors.test_description: Create fixture plugins A<->B cyclic dependency, each with observable import/onLoad side effects. Run loader and assert: cycle error includes both plugin names, neither plugin import side effect occurs, and no onLoad is called.

behaviors.description: A plugin's onLoad hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is specifically a sequencing contract between module import execution and lifecycle callback invocation. Unit-testing hook invoker cannot catch import-order regressions. REAL-INFRA: requires real module import behavior (local runtime/files), but not subprocess/network. MOCK-HIDE: mocking import would likely force a fixed order and hide real timing/ordering bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because no real external infrastructure boundary is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Fixture plugin entry module sets exported/side-effect flag during top-level execution; onLoad asserts that flag is already set and records pass/fail. Run loader and assert onLoad sees post-import state, proving import+execution precedes hook call.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its onLoad hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is error propagation across dependency graph execution (failed plugin, dependents blocked, independents continue). Needs resolver + executor + error handling together. REAL-INFRA: no network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: isolated unit tests can miss incorrect stop-the-world behavior or incorrect dependent filtering. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because this is in-process control flow and dependency handling.
behaviors.test_description: Set up fixtures: plugin F throws in onLoad; plugin D depends on F; plugin I is independent. Run loader and assert: F failure is reported, D is skipped/not initialized, I still initializes successfully, and process does not abort entire load sequence.
